3963d5f454ba1b58.gist: The core dataset table: 642 rows of matched (C/C++ source, x86-64 disassembly) function pairs from zlib, each tagged with compiler, optimization level, and dedup hash.

3f7cc85ea0c20d83.gist: A failure log of 50 translation units that could not be compiled during the build sweep, recording the repo, file, opt level, and compiler error reason.

49ace683507b76d3.gist: An empty provenance ledger meant to track repositories processed in a future large-scale GitHub crawl (Phase 2), recording URL, commit SHA, license, status, and pairs contributed.

d44260d5c3be133f.gist: The core dataset: 84,974 rows, each pairing a C source function with its disassembled assembly, plus metadata (repo, file_path, func_name, signature, lang, arch, opt_level, obj_format, compiler) and source/asm/pair content hashes. This is the training corpus of source-to-disassembly pairs.

4fed327a71c403fa.gist: 13,688 rows recording source files that failed to compile during dataset generation, keyed by repo/file_path/opt_level with a free-text reason holding the compiler error output (missing headers, invalid conversions, unsupported vector intrinsics, etc.). This is the failure/rejection log complementing the successful pairs.

507ce55ac3f25226.gist: 116 rows describing the upstream GitHub repositories the dataset is built from: url, commit_sha, license, processing status (done/queued/failed), n_pairs produced, processed_at timestamp, failure reason, and stars. This is the provenance/catalog of source projects.
